channel.title: New local problems on FixMyStreet
channel.description: The latest local problems reported by users

item.title: Rubbish again!!!, 2nd July
item.description:
This is the best place to dump your.rubbish!!! In here is all the time rubbish. People leaving here different kinds of rubbish from small one to the big one as furniture. To clean that is always at least 2 weeks time. Thats ridiculous!! Where our money goes to.!!! For sure once in a month they clean our street just to say that Yees we clean our streets.Ha,ha ha....All People who are in the Council are same dirty as people who put rubbish in our street insted to put it in the rubbish bins!!!!

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Eversley Cross, North End, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.description:
Broken bed base has been left at the back of 40 for the last few weeks.

item.title: Large branch fell off tree, tree has never been pruned, 2nd July
item.description:
Branch fell off tree recently onto car. Tree has never been included in regular pruning. Branches are resting above communication wires, will eventually cause them to break.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 25, Minster Road, Sundridge, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Rubbish, 2nd July

item.title: Rubbish, 2nd July
item.description:
On Perry Street along way from one round about to the big roundabout near Ford, there is plenty of rubbish small big pieces. Big pieces are some packing foil which can fly to the approaching cars make a huge problem if there are fly to their windscreen and will obstruct their view. Then car accident will be. No one is cleaning and taking care of this area and most of the Bexley area. Thats filthy dirty place,which isn't to nice to live. Little kids are walking to school and need to see this mess. Shame!!!

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Perry Street, Crayford, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: By 10 Shottery Close, 2nd July
item.description:
Drain cover has sunk and is a trip hazzard

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Shottery Close, Mottingham, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Repeated hazardous commercial waste dumping – razor blades on pavement near schools, 2nd July
item.description:
This is the third time I have reported this issue because it has not been resolved. A business operating as Tristan Beauty Salon is repeatedly leaving black bags of commercial waste on the pavement beside the public bins instead of disposing of it correctly. The bags contain a mixture of barber waste, including large amounts of cut hair, general waste, food waste and used razor blades. The bags are regularly ripped open by foxes, scattering the contents across the pavement. This leaves used razor blades spread across the footway, creating a serious risk of injury to pedestrians. This location is particularly concerning because it is close to local primary and secondary schools, and many children and families walk past during the school run. I have personally seen razor blades scattered across the pavement while taking my child through the area. This is an ongoing issue, not a one-off incident. Previous reports have been marked as resolved, but the same hazardous waste continues to be left outside the premises. Please investigate this business’s commercial waste disposal practices and take appropriate enforcement action to prevent this recurring public safety hazard.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 44, Grove Vale, East Dulwich, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Rubbish still not cleared, 2nd July
item.description:
Job Ref 9637144. I notice that this job has still not been resolved. We constantly have this problem because it’s between the two councils and they leave it one another to resolve. This has been outstanding now for weeks. I will now refer once again to local MPs
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Lodge Hill, East Wickham, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Grass seeds need cutting - out of control, 2nd July
item.description:
The grass hasn’t been cut in atleast 6 weeks meaning grass seeds have grown out of control causing harm to many dogs in the area and in turn costing residents so much money to get our dogs comfortable again. Grass seeds are deadly to our animals and can cause deafness when lodged in the ear. I’ve personally had to have 3 grass seed removals at the vet, my groomer has removed several that have pierced my boys chest and talking to local residents and the vets in the area they are seeing atleast 6 cases a week. The grass needs to be cut more regularly and cleared up afterwards as the grass seeds left on the floor cause harm too!! Thank you
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 13, Fairway, Poverest, Orpington, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Two large fridge/freezers dumped, 2nd July
item.description:
Two large fridge/freezers dumped on Fountain Drive, very close to junction with Rock Hill. Completely blocking pavement (I had to go into the cycle lane with my buggy).
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Fountain Drive, Dulwich, Greater London
